title: Perihelion v1.4.3
content:
This Perihelion pass turned into a checkpoint about stability and navigation, not a final visual redesign. We explored several tighter gallery-toolbar and staging-header arrangements, but the lasting gains were the ones that made state, sharing, and staging more durable without forcing the interface into a layout that did not feel settled yet.

What was accomplished:

  • URL and history state now carry real context: the app can preserve folder path, page, image height, item limit, filters, search, selection mode, and open image state in the URL so back/forward navigation behaves more like a real workspace instead of resetting the session.
  • Selection work can survive mode changes better: selected items can now persist through selected/staging transitions using a session-backed working set draft, which makes the export flow sturdier when moving between the gallery and staging view.
  • Share and app routing were tightened: share-code parsing now avoids false matches like home, generated share links point back into the app cleanly, and the top-left Perihelion wordmark now returns to the main app workspace rather than the landing page.
  • Staging layout kept the clearer rule order: rename rules stay visually ahead of resize and compression controls, which makes the export surface easier to read while keeping the existing processing options intact.

What did not stay:

  • We tried multiple condensed toolbar arrangements for the gallery options and the staging action row.
  • Those experiments were intentionally rolled back instead of being forced through, because the stable GitHub-backed configuration was clearer than the intermediate layouts we tested.

What was kept:

  • The existing browsing model, share workflow, and image-height controls remained intact.
  • The new stateful navigation and working-set behavior stayed in place.
  • The staging/export panel kept the more readable ordering improvements without turning into a full visual overhaul.
